lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a question or two I would like to ask you.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Of course. Go ahead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. My name's [player name].

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Ahhh, good day young adventurer. I can't stop to talk right now I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Ahhh, good day young adventurer. A good day indeed! Sylas is the name. It is lucky for you that you met me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why, what do you mean?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Well it just so happens, [player name], that I have something you simply must have!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? What is it?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Beans, my friend! Beans!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would I want beans exactly? I mean, beans are good, of course, but I'm pretty sure I can get them anywhere.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Ahhh, but not beans like this, young [player name]. No, not at all! These are 'magic' beans.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok then. So what do they do exactly?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: An excellent question! Excellent indeed! What do they do? What do they do? Yes, yes! A most excellent question!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: O...kay. So, are you going to tell me what they do?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: You must plant them in the ground. Water them well. Then the magic will work, it's...er...magic...yes! Plant them, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, can I have the beans then?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: All in good time, my excitable young friend. All in good time.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Few things in this world are free, you know, and beans - magical or otherwise - are no exception!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, how much gold do you want for the beans, if I decide to buy them?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Ah, well you see now I am a collector of rare and unusual trinkets and objects. Not much in value, but very important to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I see. Ok then.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Right, well I think that for magic beans, I will need something magical back and I know of just the right items that would help my collection.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, so if I get these items, you will trade them for the beans. Is that right?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Yes! Excellent! You do catch on quick! I can see that we will become great business partners indeed. Oh, yes. Yes indeed! Hmm-ha! Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What items do you need me to find then - assuming I still want those beans of course. Not that I do, but I might.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Well, there are two. The first item should be nice and simple for you to get for me. I want a griffin feather. Now, this is not just any feather, this one is special.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's so special about it?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: There is a griffin named Grimgnash who lives in a nest on one of the peaks of White Wolf Mountain. It is one of his feathers that I want.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: But although this task should be easy, you must be careful [player name]. Grimgnash has a very bad temper and even sharper claws!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks for the advice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you need the feather for?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: I told you, [player name]. It is for my collection of magical items and trinkets, weird and wonderful thereof.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thereof?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Yes, I was quite happy with that word. I think it went rather well in that last sentence, don't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess. If you say so.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Yes. Excellent! Yes. Now...er...was there anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I find Grimgnash again?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: When he's not chewing on some poor soul's bones or raining terror on an unsuspecting village, he can be found in his nest, atop White Wolf Mountain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me...say what now? He chews people's bones? I thought you said this was an easy task?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Oh, I daresay he has to eat sometimes. But I don't think he'd take too much fancy for you. Not much meat on you at all. Oh dear me, no!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, I'm not sure that was even a compliment.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: I'm sure it was meant to be! Don't worry, [player name], you'll be fine. Now was there anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the other item I need to find?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Right, well, the second item is this. I know of a dwarf named Rupert the Beard who has a very fine dwarven helmet. I'd like you to see if you can obtain it for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok. Where can I find him?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Travel north-east from here and between the Goblin Village and Ice Mountain you will find Rupert in a tower there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, that seems simple enough. I'll get the helmet for you as well.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Excellent! Yes, yes. Now was there anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any weird items in your collection?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Haha! Weird items? Oh yes. Yes indeed! Yes. Many items. Many items. Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm...I see. Well, can you tell me about any of them?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Hmm. Well let's see now. I have the second eye of a mutant two-eyed cyclops. That was a rare find indeed! And at a tidy price too!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A two-eyed cyclops? Uh...wouldn't that be a normal giant perhaps?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Whatever do you mean? How many mutant two-eyed cyclops do you think there are out there? Not many, I'd wager!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm...no, I guess not. Well, what else do you have?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Ah, well I'm glad you asked. My most prized possession to date: Through my connections, I managed to acquire a horn-less Unicorn!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? That's amazing! A unicorn without a horn?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Yes. Yes indeed! Indeed yes! And I'm very proud of it, too! It was an exceptionally lucky find, I must say!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I expect so. Well, on to other things for now then, I guess.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Of course. Was there anything else you wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should be off, I think.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Excellent, excellent! Best of luck then, my friend. I'll be right here waiting for you to return.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh...hello?

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Human? Human come to Grimgnash? Grimgnash pleased! Grimgnash hungry!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, wait! I didn't come to be eaten!

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: What human come for then? Grimgnash tired and hungry. Be quick, human. Answer Grimgnash before Grimgnash eats your legs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh...uh...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I heard you were a great and mighty Griffin!

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Hah! Human hears right about Grimgnash! Grimgnash great and mighty griffin!

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Now what human want? Grimgnash tired and can't sleep. Wolves make awful howling racket. Keep Grimgnash up all night.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I can help you get to sleep in some way. Is there anything I can do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have come for your magic feather!

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Grimgnash's feather? Human come to steal feather? How dare human! Puny, scrawny little morsel! Grimgnash should eat human where human stands!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any quests for me?

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Quests? Quests? Human morsel wants a quest from Grimgnash? Human think Grimgnash is drunken dwarf or lazy ork? Grimgnash not gives quests!

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Grimgnash tired and hungry and will eat little bite-size human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Argh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look what I've got!

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Grargk! Grimgnash not care for stupid human things! Grimgnash hungry!

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Human toothpick dares to approach Grimgnash again after what human just say? Why human disturb Grimgnash? Human have death wish?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, wait! I was wondering if perhaps I could help you at all. You said you couldn't sleep. Is there anything I can do?

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Human help Grimgnash sleep? This good. Grimgnash let human help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok. What can I do?

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Hmm. When Grimgnash little grifflet, Grimgnash's mother-griff used to tell bedtime story to Grimgnash. That always help Grimgnash sleep.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you want me to tell you a bed-time story, is that right?

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Yes! Grimgnash like stories!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, well I'm sure that shouldn't be too hard. I know lots of stories!

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Good. Grimgnash like stories. Lots of death and fire and destruction! Grimgnash ready!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Death and fire?...ok then. Let me see...

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: And make story good. Otherwise, if no sleep, Grimgnash more hungry!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There once was a graveyard filled with undead.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Graveyard is good place for story. Is undeads all horrible and ugly?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh...yes...yes I suppose they could be.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Grimgnash like this story. Go on. Go on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There lived a skeleton named Grimgnash.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Grimgnash? That Grimgnash's name! Not stupid skeleton's name! How skeleton have Grimgnash's name?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, wait! Sorry! I got that bit wrong there. I was thinking of your name. My mistake! What I meant was...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There lived a skeleton named Skullrot.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Oh yes! Skullrot. That great name! Grimgnash like that name. Maybe Grimgnash call himself Grimgnash Skullrotter? What human think?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I...er..I'm sure nobody would disagree with you.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Yes. Grimgnash Skullrotter. Grimgnash like that very much. What happens next? This good story...for human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Skullrot was happy!

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Why Skullrot happy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh...he was happy because...er...he was dead?

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Because he dead? That stupid. How someone happy if they dead? Stupid human tell stupid story! Raaargh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Skullrot was sad!

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Sad? Why he sad?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh...I guess because he was...er...dead?

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: What human mean, dead? How Skullrot dead if he alive and walking around? That stupid!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't think you understand what a skeleton is, exactly...

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Now human call Grimgnash stupid? Human is stupid! Human is tasty morsel! Grimgnash likes tasty morsels!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Argh...!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Skullrot was angry!

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: What Skullrot angry about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh...he was angry because...er...he had a headache?

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: A headache? That daft idea. Skeletons have nothing in head to ache. Grimgnash think human is making story up. Not real story like mother-griff used to tell.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Grimgnash just eat human instead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oww!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Skullrot was insane!

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Yes, insane! Grimgnash like that word! Grimgnash mother always say Grimgnash's daddy insane. Grimgnash's daddy eat own claws. Grimgnash laughed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's...lovely. Uh...shall I continue?

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Yes! Grimgnash liking this story now! Go on!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, then. So Skullrot was completely insane. He was off his rocker. One noodle short of a bowl of spaghetti. Not quite all there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anyway, he was out haunting his graveyard one day when he came across a small gnome.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Grimgnash not like gnomes. They stringy and chewy and they not have much meat for Grimgnash's belly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, I'm sorry to hear that. Anyway, this poor gnome had somehow got his foot stuck under a fallen gravestone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Skullrot lifted the gravestone off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And the gnome ran away as quick as his little feet could carry him.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: What?! Gnome snack gets away? That dumb. How Skullrot eat? Stupid human can't tell good story!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, wait...!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Skullrot left the gnome there to die.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the gnome, thinking this was his chance, found a log to lever the stone off his foot and he scarpered away quickly.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: What?! Gnome snack gets away? That stupid. That not good story! Grimgnash only feel more hungry now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, wait, I got it wrong! Wait...!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Skullrot laughed at the gnome.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In fact, he laughed so hard that he fell backwards into an empty grave and was never seen again. The end.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Graah! That good stor...wait. What happen to gnome?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eh? Oh, uh...well without Skullrot there he was able to get himself free again and ran away, vowing never to return to the graveyard ever again.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: What?! Little bite-size gnome runs away? That not good ending! Gnome should be mashed by something like...something like Grimgnash! Stupid human get eaten now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, wait, I got it wrong! Wait...!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Skullrot hungrily grabbed the gnome's hair.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Skullrot wrenched the gnome out with one hand, pulling hard on his hair. The poor gnome's foot snapped out of the gravestone and went crack as his foot dislocated!

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Raahahaha! Stupid gnome! Serves gnome right for being stringy and chewy! What happen next?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, the poor gnome - Nobby was his name - was in terrible pain and fear as to what would happen to him next.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Grimgnash not care what gnome's name is. Grimgnash not give food names. Does human call a carrot Jasper or something? Tell rest of story!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, ok. Anyway, the gnome was in awful pain, so Skullrot took him back to his home in his grave where he...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Locked him in an iron cage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Luckily, the bars were built for a human sized person and so the gnome quickly slipped through them and ran - painfully - out the door.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The gnome headed straight out the graveyard, glad to still be alive and never wanting to return.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Stringy gnome gets away? Stupid human make Grimgnash more hungry with story like that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, stop! Arrgh...!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Put him into a pot for dinner.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Skullrot was making a stew and had decided that the gnome would taste wonderful in it.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Ah! Grimgnash likes gnome stew. Just like Grimgnash used to eat at home. Mmmm!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: However, Skullrot had filled the pot up too much, and so while the skeleton's back was turned, the gnome swam to the edge of the pot and up, over the side.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: With a little shout of glee, the gnome hopped on one leg straight out of the door and fled as fast as his little foot could hop him!

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Gnome gets away? What kind of story that? Grimgnash think he eat human now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Argh...!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Started to strangle the poor gnome.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The gnome struggled with the cold boney fingers that were trying to end his life until he managed to pry himself free!

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Ohhh! This getting good! Grimgnash like this. (Yawn!) Grimgnash feels sleepy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, it was all the little gnome could do to crawl painfully across the floor away from Skullrot, who was busy getting a pot to put the gnome in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then, when all seemed lost for the little gnome, he suddenly saw something that might save his little life!

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Grargh! What? What? What little gnome see? He must not escape! Grimgnash hungry to think of it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, ok! Don't worry, we're almost done.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Grmmm. Good. Grimgnash getting sleepy...(yawn).

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, then. The gnome looked up and he saw what might be his best chance to get out alive!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He saw a hole he could just fit through.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As he crawled forward two yellow slitted eyes appeared in the gloom before him. He froze in fear!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Skullrot did not get his dinner that night, but something else...something much worse...did. The end.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what did you think, Grimgnash?

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Zzzzzz....zzzz....zzz...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He saw a big axe leaning on the wall.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He crawled painfully, oh so painfully over to the axe. He could use it to kill Skullrot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As he grasped the axe he realised it was a lot heavier than it looked! In fact much heavier indeed! He couldn't hold it or stop it falling off the wall!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: SCHNUNK!!

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: What happened? Human tell Grimgnash!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As the axe fell, the poor gnome could not get out of the way in time and the razor sharp blade fell CRACK! And it killed him instantly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And so Skullrot was pleased to find that he had a nice dinner for that night and sat down for a big supper! The end.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you think, Grimgnash?

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Zzzzzz....zzzz....zzz...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He saw some bones lying in the corner.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He quickly remembered that if he buried them he could get his prayer up and so might be able to cast a heal spell on his legs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He dragged his painful body over to the bones and buried them all - there was quite a large pile - in the floor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All of a sudden, he remembered a good spell to heal himself from his injuries. He started to recite the prayer quickly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just then, a huge zombie appeared out of nowhere and mumbled something about his bones being disturbed! The zombie crunched his fist down on the gnome's head.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The gnome cried out in terror and realised that all was lost! He could not get away now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And so, Skullrot decided to invite the zombie to dinner as he never usually had guests.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then the two of them laughed and joked merrily throughout the night with their stomachs full of a hearty dinner! The end.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what did you think, Grimgnash?

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Zzzzzz....zzzz....zzz...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He saw a small sandwich on the floor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He pulled himself over to it. In his desperation he thought to himself, there's a sandwich here. A sandwich! Maybe the skeleton will eat that instead?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He quickly grasped the sandwich in his little hands. Strangely, in his panic-stricken state, he noticed that it was long and tubular shaped.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What an odd thing to notice, he thought. And then all of a sudden he heard an almighty voice boom out of nowhere!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'SANDWICHES!!!'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Oi!', said the voice. 'I didn't say you could take that!' And with that, there was an almighty crunch on his little gnome head, and that was the last thing he felt.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Skullrot sat down for the night and had a lovely big dinner, followed by many baguette sandwiches. Not triangle ones, no, no! The end.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you think, Grimgnash?

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Zzzzzz....zzzz....zzz...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Locked the gnome in the attic to die.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fortunately for the gnome, Skullrot forgot that he lived in a grave, so he didn't have an attic. Instead the gnome was locked outside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The gnome quickly limped away, glad to still be alive.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Gnome gets away? What kind of story that? Stupid human make Grimgnash more hungry!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Argh...!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There lived a skeleton named Boney.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Boney? That stupid name for skeleton. Of course he boney. Human think Grimgnash stupid? Human make fun of Grimgnash? Grimgnash think human look better in Grimgnash's belly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No wait...I...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There lived a skeleton named Harry.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: What kind of name Harry for skeleton? That stupid name. How many skeletons called Harry you meet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, I've not really...

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Grimgnash think Grimgnash just eat you instead of story!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There once was a king named Roland.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Gaah! This sound like boring story! Maybe Grimgnash eat you instead, yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? No! Wait, I got that bit wrong! Sorry. What I meant was...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There once was a princess named Esmerelda.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Urgh! Grimgnash hate princesses. They bones and no meat. Need eating more, Grimgnash thinks. Speak of eating, Grimgnash maybe eat you instead of story!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There once was a little bunny called Flumper.

lines.speaker: Grimgnash
lines.text: Bunnies? Bunnies!? Grimgnash hate this story! This story smell worse than Grimgnash's feet. Stupid human tell awful story! Make Grimgnash more hungry than ever!

sections.title: Trying to talk to Grimgnash after he's asleep

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, Sylas. I have Grimgnash's feather for you.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Ah! Good work. Let me take that from you then.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: I still need you to find Rupert the Beard's Helmet for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll get that next.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a question or two I would like to ask you.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Of course. Go ahead.

sections.title: (Optional) Attempting to enter the tower door

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Excuse me. You can't go in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, sorry.

sections.title: (Optional) Talking to Miazrqa before speaking with Rupert

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Oh, hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Who am I? Why, how impertinent! I am a princess and I will be spoken to like one. Now be off with you. I have no time for scruffy adventurers like yourself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh...ok. Never mind then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look what I've got!

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: I'm pretty sure I don't want that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it's mine and you can't have it anyway. So there!

sections.title: Trying to climb the crumbled wall without the thieving level

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Hey, what's that noise over there? Is someone there? Who's near my tower?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh...no...no, nothing to see here. It was just the...er...wind! Yes, the wind.

sections.title: Speaking to Rupert through the pipe

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: I say! Where on Gielinor did that voice come from?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh...hello there! Can you hear me?

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Why yes. Yes I can. My word. Is that...is that one of the gods? Guthix perhaps? Saradomin?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, no. I'm not either of them.

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Egad! You're not...Zamorak...are you? I mean, uh...begging your pardon of course, my Lord. No offence intended!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ha ha! No, I'm speaking to you through the pipe here! I'm at the bottom of your tower.

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Oh. Oh I see! Oh, yes. How clever! Remarkable idea! However did you think of that? Good show, old bean!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Rupert the Beard. At your service, old fruit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rupert the Beard?

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Yes, old ogleroot! On account of my wonderfully long bearded chin, you see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are you doing up there exactly? And how come you talk so strangely?

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: What am I doing? Well, I guess I'm stuck in a matter of speaking. Dear me, yes! I'm stuck. And I speak in the King's speech, I'll have you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? Which king?

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Which K...? Why, THE King old spud! THE King! Only one worth mentioning in my opinion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, you're not related to Sir Tiffy by any chance are you?

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: What? That namby pamby nincompoop? Not on your nelly! More fluff in the ears than sense, that man!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Well is there anything I can do to help you up there?

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: By Guthix, I know how you can help me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure. What can I do?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Hey, what's that noise over there? Is someone there?

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Ye gods! We can't talk like this anymore. You need to find a way up to speak to me privately, old boot!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why? I don't understand what's going on!

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: That woman is a princess. She's holding me prisoner. If she sees you I will be in even more trouble and who knows what she'll do to you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right! Let me have a think, then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: I say! Where on Gielinor did that voice come from?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm...it's me again. [player name]. I'm speaking in the drain pipe, remember?

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Drain pi...oh, yes! Of course! Well, hello again old cigar! How great to se...er...hear from you again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure there must be something I can do to help you up there.

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Hmm. Let me think now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure. What can I do?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Hey, what's that noise over there? Is someone there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why? I don't understand what's going on!

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: That woman is a princess. She's holding me prisoner. If she sees you I will be in even more trouble and who knows what she'll do to you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right! Let me have a think, then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I might have an idea!

sections.title: (Optional) Speaking with Miazrqa after first dialogue with Rupert

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: You there! Young scrub! Was that you making all that noise back there? Well? Speak up now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Noise? What noise? I didn't hear any noise.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Well I distinctly heard voices at least! In any case, keep away from my tower.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not very nice, are you?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: I am a princess. I don't have to be nice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought princesses were usually kind and loving and graceful?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Ha! This isn't a fairytale, you know. This is rea...well, it's life!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Well I think I had better go then.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Yes, and stay away from my dwar...uh...tower!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I might have an idea!

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Jolly good show, old bean! What do you suggest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You could try and climb down.

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Climb down? What what what? It's miles! People are like little ants from up here...oh wait...no that's just a line of ants on the wall.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's really not that far.

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Tish, posh and nonsense! You and your gangly long legs might be fine, but we dwarves are of shorter, stockier breed, old toothbrush!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you won't try it then?

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Indeed, definitely and most indubitably not! Think of something else!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could try and climb up.

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Hmm, a worthy endeavour I would say. Possibly with its merits. Yes, indeed. Now how will you get up here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could try and climb the walls.

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: I don't know if that would work, old trousers. These walls are made from very smooth and flat stone. Excellent workmanship. Simply excellent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm glad you're able to admire the architecture, but you're still stuck up there!

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Right you are, old pockets! Keep thinking! These are excellent ideas you're having!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything up there that can help?

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Not really, old spoon. There's not much up here except for me and my beard. Ha ha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait! That's it!

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: What's it, old banana?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't believe I'm going to say this, but...Rupert, Rupert, let down your beard!

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Eh? Let down my beard? Have you lost it down there, old chum? Going a bit loopy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I mean hang your beard down over the tower and I'll climb up it, ok?

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Ah ha ha! Excellent idea, old tomato! Here it comes then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can fly!

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: By Guthix and Saradomin, that's astounding! You are an absolute marvel! Can you really fly?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well...er...no, sorry, I made that last bit up!

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: I see. Not very sporting in the circumstances, was it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm, no I guess not. Sorry about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There must be something down here that might help.

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: I'm afraid I have no idea! It's a long way down as I said, so I can't see much from way up here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm telling you, it's really not that high.

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Lies! Lies! Now can you see anything down there that might help, old chicken?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really, no.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could ask the princess!

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Hang on, old underpants!...you're going to ask the princess to help you climb up this wall to see her prisoner.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, on second thought maybe that's not such a good idea after all.

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: No, indeed. Indeed, no.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I could try and ask her to let you out, perhaps? You know, get her to listen to reason.

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Reason, eh? Hmmm. Well you could give that a try.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could try speaking to the princess.

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Well, I suppose it wouldn't hurt to try, old socks! What would you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I could try and get her to listen to reason, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Reason, eh? Hmmm. Well, you could give that a try.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could kill the princess for you!

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: What? Kill the princess?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, she doesn't look so tough!

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: What ever has gotten into your head, old war wound? Kill the princess. No, no, no. We don't do that sort of thing round here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But she's kidnapped you. Surely that makes her evil?

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Well, yes of course. In a manner of speaking, yes. But...but that still doesn't give us the right to go around lopping people's heads off because they're not very nice!

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: No, there must be another way! Keep thinking!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But it would have been fun though, wouldn't it? Haha!

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Hmmm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She would never have seen it coming! Blam!

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Yes, I get the idea. Now be a sport and think of a better idea!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right!

sections.title: (Optional) Speaking to Miazrqa about letting Rupert out before entering the tower

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pardon me...?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Yes? Please make it quick, I am waiting for someone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, really? Who are you waiting for? Maybe I've met them?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: You think so? Ha! Although you'd most likely remember if you had met this person. If you must know, I am waiting for a relative of mine to arrive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, okay. Can I ask you something?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: I suppose so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I notice you have a dwarf in the tower here. I was wondering why he is locked in there?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: You are a nosy young person! Well, if you must know, he's in there because I have caught him for someone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, who's that then?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: I am not at liberty to say. In any case, this dwarf was caught spying and snooping and that sort of thing is not taken lightly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But he seems like a very nice person to me. A bit odd perhaps, but couldn't you let him go?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: No, I don't think so. I will be nicely rewarded for catching him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh go on. Please?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Hmm, well as you asked nicely. Ok then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: No! Now please leave. You're annoying me!

sections.title: Speaking to Rupert through the drain pipe after he let down his beard

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello? Rupert?

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Eh? What? Oh! You don't have to use that anymore, old spoon! Climb up to speak to me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Climb up how?

lines.speaker: Drain pipe
lines.text: Up my beard of course! It was your idea after all, old spinach.

sections.title: Speaking to Rupert in the tower

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Aha! You made it. Well done, old sausage!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, it was quite a climb, but you have a very strong beard.

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Indeed I do. Indeed I do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what can I help you with?

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Ah! Straight to the point, eh, what? Good show old carrot, good show. Now let me explain.

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: I'm in a bit of a fix right now. You see, the princess down there has this horrible relative who happens to be a witch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A witch? Let me guess, would this witch be her evil stepmother?

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Stepmother? Ho! Good lord, no. No king in his right mind would marry a hag like that! No, this is her evil second-cousin, twice-removed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Go on.

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Right, well a young gnome friend of mine by the name of Winkin recently disappeared whilst delivering ogleroots to the market in Draynor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? What happened to him?

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: I don't know exactly, but someone told me he was last seen talking to a witch before he disappeared.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, so how can I help?

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: My investigations brought me to our friend the princess down there, old pinata. She's related to this witch, as I mentioned. I was...you know...showing a bit of interest in her to get her to give me information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then what happened?

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: She invited me back to her 'tower', saying she would tell me all I wanted to know, and then as I walked in the door she slammed it shut behind me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodness me! How rude!

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Yes, indeed! Rude indeed! Then, as I was hammering on the door I heard a hissing sound and smelt something strange. The next thing I remember is waking up to find my armour gone!

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: That wench must have taken it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, let me go and talk to her for you, then.

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Excellent! Good show, old spice! Off you go then.

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Ahh, you're back. Have you spoken to the princess yet, old mushroom?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, no I haven't had a chance yet. I'll be back when I have.

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Right you are then, old chestnut.

sections.title: (Optional) Attempting to leave the tower through the trapdoor
(Optional) Talking to Miazrqa if you walked away mid-dialogue

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please can you repeat that last bit? A flock of noisy birds flew overhead there and I couldn't hear you.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: I see. How ghastly. Let me continue.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Now as I was saying, my second-cousin, twice-removed is coming to collect the dwarf. She will find out why he's been snooping around. She has most...persuasive methods.

sections.title: (Optional) Talking to Miazrqa again before leaving

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just have another question before I look for your pendant.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: If you must, but you are wasting time.

sections.title: (Optional) Talking to Rupert before looking for Miazrqa's pendant

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Rupert.

lines.speaker: Rupert
lines.text: Ah! Welcome back, old curtains! How did it go with the old princess down there? Did she agree to let me out?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sort of. She's asked me to do something for her first.

lines.speaker: Rupert
lines.text: Oh, right. What was it then, old parrot?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have to find a gold pendant of hers that she lost in that witch's house in Taverley.

lines.speaker: Rupert
lines.text: By all the gods! You have to go in there? You must be careful, [player name]. She's a wily one that witch, from what I've heard. No doubt there's all sorts of weird magics over there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry. I'll be careful. I've been in there once before, so I know what's there.

lines.speaker: Rupert
lines.text: You've been in there? Goodness me! What did you see? Did you see a gnome in there anywhere?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think so. I'm sure I would have remembered. I was looking for a ball for a boy who had lost it in the witch's garden.

lines.speaker: Rupert
lines.text: Hmm, he was probably lucky that was all he lost with all that weird magic going on, eh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, quite. I'll see what I can find out though.

lines.speaker: Rupert
lines.text: Ah! Good show, old kebab! I'll wait here for you. But don't be long. Who knows what that princess will do to me next!

sections.title: Playing the correct tune at the piano
Upon entering the room with the mouse hole

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nnnngh!

sections.title: (Optional) Talking to Rupert the Beard before handing the pendant

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Rupert.

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Ah! [player name], old pantaloons! Good to see you, good to see you. What news?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Princess Miazrqa has agreed to free you.

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Princess Marzipan, eh? What a mouthful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Miazrqa. It's a silent Q.

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Ah, I see. Good show. Say, did you find out anything about my missing friend?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did, I think. But it's not good news. The witch has your friend locked up in a cage, down under the house itself.

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Good gods! That's awful! I won't have all this kidnapping nonsense! And to think we're supposed to be a civilised land!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will you do?

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: I'll think of something. In the meantime, you still need to get me out of here. Maybe you should see if Miazrqa is ready to let me out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. I'm on it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found your pendant, Princess Miazrqa!

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Oh, how wonderful! I'm so grateful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm, your second-cousin has some very strange...creatures she keeps.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Oh yes. Those are her little darlings. I shudder to think what she might do if something were to happen to any of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... Really?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Oh yes. Why, only last month one of them got out for a wander and some horrid farmer attacked it with his ear!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: With his ear?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Indeed! Tried to choke the poor thing with it. Anyway, my cousin went into a frightful rage and turned that wretched farmer into one of his own chickens. We had a lovely roast meal that day. Happy memories...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I feel a little ill...

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Oh dear, I hope you're not coming down with something.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Now, can I have the pendant please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you agree to let Rupert go free?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Of course. The pendant means more to me than anything.

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: By Saradomin! My armour was on the stairs up there!

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: That's better!

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: FREEDOM!

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Aha! Sweet freedom! It's good to be alive, old sweet shop! Thank you for freeing me, [player name].

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]. You've done a great service for me and all the dwarves and gnomes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just happy I could help...hey! What happened to your long beard? Where did it go?

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Hah! Don't fret, old penny whistle! It's still here. The front of my armour was specially crafted to allow my beard to fold up inside. Do you see?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes! I can see it now. That's pretty clever!

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Indeed it is, old cheese! Now allow me to reward you in some way. All I have is my armour, but you are welcome to take any piece of it that you wish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please could I have the helmet?

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Of course, of course. Here you go, old pumpkin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what will you be doing now, Rupert?

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Thanks to you I can carry on my investigations. Good show, old brussels sprout!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm glad I could help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Princess Miazrqa, I found out what your cousin is up to.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Really? You know, I have always wondered. She's ever so secretive and never tells me anything. I was hoping she might tell me when I saw her next.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you didn't know that she kidnaps poor defenceless gnomes?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: What?! Never! That's absolutely awful! How could she? Oh, that's just so...so...mean!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought so too.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: It's quite hard to believe. Do you have any proof?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. There is a gnome in her basement right now!

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: How horrid! Well, when I see her I will tell her exactly what I think. Thank you for telling me all this, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, Rupert - the dwarf here - is trying to put a stop to what is going on. I am sure he could use your help.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Really? You may tell him that I will help him in any way I can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rupert, I told Miazrqa about what the witch was doing and she was horrified by it.

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: I'm not surprised, old kiwi. It's a pretty ghastly business with all this kidnapping gnomes and dwarves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. Miazrqa has said she would like to help you with your investigations and that you can use this tower as your base of operations if you want.

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: Really? That's just fantastic! You are certainly full of surprises, [player name]. Good show!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I had better be going for now. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Rupert the Beard
lines.text: And to you, my friend. And to you.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Thanks again for finding my pendant. I felt so lost without it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're welcome. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pardon me, your ladyship.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Ah! What nice manners. It's so hard to find decently mannered young people these days. My name is Princess Miazrqa.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pleased to meet you, Princess Miarz... er... Mizaz... Miquipzi... Marzipan?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Miazrqa. With a silent Q.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's an unusual name. Where is it from?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: My birth certificate. Now. how can I be of help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see there is an embarrassed-looking dwarf in the tower there.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Yes, that's right. I'm waiting for him to be collected by someone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your first-cousin, once removed?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: No. You shouldn't guess answers like that. It's not very becoming. She is my second-cousin, twice removed and she is also a very powerful witch who could turn you into something nasty!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gosh! Sorry, please continue.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Now as I was saying, my second-cousin, twice-removed is coming to collect the dwarf. She will find out why he's been snooping around. She has most...persuasive methods.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, that's actually why I came to speak with you. I was wondering if you couldn't let Rupert go? He's a nice fellow and I can't think he'd have done anything wrong.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Ha! That won't matter to my cousin. She values her privacy above all else, you see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Surely there must be something that will change your mind? Is there anything you want?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Hmmm...well I suppose there is something you can do for me that might make me change my mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, what's that?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: I was at my second-cousin's house just the other day when I suddenly dropped a very valuable pendant that I was wearing. The chain must have broken, you see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, go on.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Well, no sooner had it touched the ground when all of a sudden a mouse ran out from a hole in the wall. It took my pendant in its teeth and disappeared back inside its hole.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: As I'm sure you will believe, I was most upset as the pendant was a gift from my mother. I simply must get it back somehow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you would like me to find it for you?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Oh, yes please! It means so much to me. If you are able to find the pendant for me, I will let your dwarf friend go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well. I will find it for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is your second-cousin's house?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: It's not far to the west of here, in Taverley village. It has a beautiful long garden - you can't miss it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey! I do know that house! I rescued some little kid's ball from there!

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: That was you? Oh, my cousin was absolutely furious when she found out about that! She went on about it for ages and even made her hair turn a quite ghastly green colour!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Well I suggest you be extra careful when you look for my pendant then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does your pendant look like?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: It's a lovely gold pendant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is it particularly valuable?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Only to me really. It's not worth much and isn't magical, but it has a lot of sentimental value. It was my mother's pendant. She gave it to me on my tenth birthday.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where are you the princess of exactly?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: I am the daughter of a great and powerful king. In a land far, far away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How far, exactly?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Far, far.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. What's the place called?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Asgarnia.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Asgarnia? Aren't we in Asgarnia?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Well...yes. I suppose, but that doesn't mean it's not far, far away from somewhere else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh...I see.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: I am the great niece of King Vallance himself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How impressive!

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Yes, it is, isn't it? Now was there anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need a key for the house.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Of course. You may have one of my spare keys.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks very much.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Don't be silly. You have one already in your bag. Goodness me, how ever will you find my pendant with a memory like that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, yes. Sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should be off, I think.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Very well. Good luck with finding my pendant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your second-cousin, once removed?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: No. You shouldn't guess answers like that. It's not very becoming. She is my second-cousin, twice removed and she is also a very powerful witch who could turn you into something nasty!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your first-cousin, twice removed?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: No. You shouldn't guess answers like that. It's not very becoming. She is my second-cousin, twice removed and she is also a very powerful witch who could turn you into something nasty!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your second-cousin, twice removed?

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: Why, yes! That was a very clever guess indeed! She's actually a very powerful witch. Now allow me to reward you in some way as I am feeling generous.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: As I said, my cousin is a witch but she is also a lover of music. She has a lovely gramophone and she keeps a piano in her basement - why? I have no idea - but you may find it interesting to examine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's so special about it? I've seen pianos all over the place.

lines.speaker: Miazrqa
lines.text: I don't really know. I know my cousin goes on about that piano a lot, but I've played on it many times and I can't figure out why she likes it so much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Thank you for that...er...useful knowledge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I managed to get Rupert's helmet for you.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Excellent, [player name]! I'll take that as well. I hope all this work wasn't too difficult.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Difficult? You have no idea! There were huge creatures trying to eat me, other creatures trying to tear me apart, witches, gnomes in distress, princesses, and I had to deal with them all!

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Goodness me! I'm glad I sent you instead of going myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Er...I mean...I knew sending you was the right thing to do. I was sure you could handle anything that came up. Now then, the beans...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, that's what all this was about wasn't it? Some beans! They must be worth a lot for this much trouble!

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Actually, the beans are almost worthless.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Worthless? So why did I go to all of this trouble for you? I'm not particularly happy about that.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Calm down, my young, hot-headed friend. There was a good reason for getting these beans.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Now that you have proven yourself worthy of being a competent and trustworthy treasure collector, I can tell the real use for the beans.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: As I said before, these beans are magical. There is a specially prepared mound of earth to the south-east of Taverley.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Plant the beans there and then be sure to water them well. You will need to have some knowledge of farming for this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, so then what do I do?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: When the beans are planted and watered, they will grow into an enormous plant of some sort. I'm not sure what exactly. At the top of this plant is our prize.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Our prize? What exactly is this prize?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: The ultimate collectible item, my dear friend. It is rumoured that in a land of clouds where none dare to tread lest they plummet to the earth, lies a goblin made of gold...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A golden goblin?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Yes. Yes, indeed! A golden goblin. Legend tells of a time when goblins discovered gold in an abandoned dwarf mine and began to mine and smelt it. What for, nobody knows. Something evil, I'd wager.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: The goblins produced great smelting works, overseen by a particularly smart goblin with a head for business. Gubblebak was his name. He was shrewd, cunning and he loved gold...

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: He worked his fellow goblins day and night to produce more and more gold, until finally they could stand his hard labour no more and they mutinied.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They mutinied?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: That's right. They turned on him. No-one is sure about the exact events, but somehow Gubblebak fell into one of the smelting pots of liquid gold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's horrible! Was he killed?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Well now, goblins are tough little creatures and he managed to somehow crawl out of the pot covered in hot, molten gold.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: He was smart indeed, but even smart people do foolish things. He threw himself into a nearby water trough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But surely that's what anyone would have done? Why is that a bad thing?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Ahh, well you see...when you take hot liquid metal and cool it instantly, it becomes rock hard straight away. That's how blacksmiths make their weapons and armour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I see. So Gubblebak became hard as a rock?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: That's right. And that's the way he stayed ever afterwards. So the legend says, at least. And the cloud area will be waiting for you when you have planted the beans to find this golden goblin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So let me see if I have this straight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to plant the beans in an earth mound south-east of here, water them, let them grow into some big plant and then climb the plant to get to a cloudy area, find a golden goblin and bring it back to you?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Exactly right! You've got it. Now did I mention about the giant?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The...giant?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Yes, that's right. The giant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm pretty sure you didn't mention any giants! What giant? Now would be a good time to mention it if you were going to!

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Oh right, sorry. Must have slipped my mind. There's a bit of a large-ish giant up there. Uh...shouldn't be anything to worry about. But...uh...I think he might have the goblin, you see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how exactly do I get the goblin then?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Well, you're probably going to have to kill him for it. But...but a big strong adventurer like yourself shouldn't have any trouble whatsoever with that, I'm sure!

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Here are the beans as promised.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok then, to recap. I need to plant the beans over there, water them, let them grow into some big plant, climb the plant to get to a cloudy area, kill a giant, find a golden goblin and bring it back to you?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Oh, well done! You do pay attention! Good luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks...I think I'm going to need it.

sections.title: Asking Sylas about the bean again

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: So then. Do you want to run through what you need to do for me again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok then, to recap: I need to plant the beans, water them, let them grow into some big plant, climb the plant to get to a cloudy area, kill a giant, find a golden goblin and bring it back to you?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Oh, well done! You do pay attention! Good luck!

sections.title: Reading the signpost next to the Earth Mound
Watering the mound before planting the bean
Planting the bean in the Earth Mound

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Woah!

sections.title: (Optional) Talking to Sylas after growing the beanstalk

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've grown the beans into a huge stalk, as you asked. What am I supposed to do now?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Hmm, have you climbed up the stalk yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Very well. Did you find the giant?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Right then. Did you defeat him and find the golden goblin?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Excellent! Well done. Now bring me the golden goblin and we're almost done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm...what happens if I forget to pick up the golden goblin after defeating the giant and leave?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Well, I expect it will still be waiting up there for you when you go back up. It's highly unlikely anyone would risk going up there to steal it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Right then, that's your next task. You will need to defeat that giant to get the golden goblin from him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: He wasn't there? This is highly irregular. Are you sure you looked properly? I expect he'd be hard to miss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and have another look.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Right, then you will need to climb the stalk and find the golden goblin for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find it?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: I think a big giant has it at the top of the stalk. You will need to get it from him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. I'm on my way.

lines.speaker: Glod
lines.text: Gods, give Glod strength!

lines.speaker: Glod
lines.text: You wouldn't like Glod when Glod angry!

lines.speaker: Glod
lines.text: Glod bash!

lines.speaker: Glod
lines.text: Glod smash!

lines.speaker: Glod
lines.text: Stupid clouds make Glod angry!

lines.speaker: Glod
lines.text: Grind your bones!

lines.speaker: Glod
lines.text: Why everyone attack Glod?

lines.speaker: Glod
lines.text: Flee, pesky mortal!

lines.speaker: Glod
lines.text: You puny human. Come to Glod!

lines.speaker: Glod
lines.text: GLOD SMASH!

lines.speaker: Glod
lines.text: Glod not die so easily!

sections.title: Speaking to Sylas after defeating Glod

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sylas! I found the golden goblin!

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Excellent work, Player! Now, that giant will likely have friends. It is too dangerous to leave that stalk up there like that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, so what should we do?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: First, let me take that goblin from you. Next we need to cut down that beanstalk as soon as possible. I hope you're a good woodcutter!

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Find an axe. Any axe should do. Then cut down that stalk as soon as you can before another giant finds out what you have done and comes for us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right!

sections.title: Asking Sylas how to cut down the stock

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you remind me what I am doing now?

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: You've forgotten already? Did you get hit on the head by that giant? Do you have amnesia?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I just can't remember what I am supposed to do.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Find an axe. Any axe should do. Then cut down that stalk as soon as you can before another giant finds out what you have done and comes for us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right!

sections.title: Trying to chop down the stalk without an axe
Chopping the beanstalk without shrinking it

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cool!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Timber!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes!

sections.title: Using axe on the stalk after it's chopped
Using any item besides axe on the stalk

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Have you cut down the stalk?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, it's done.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Good work, [player name]! At last we can relax. We've completed our task and you have done me a great service!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm glad I could help.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Now, let me reward you. You may have wondered what possible use I could have had for Rupert's helmet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It did indeed seem like an odd item to look for.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Ah, yes! However, tucked inside the helmet is a tiny bottle with a very special potion. It was made by a witch, here in Taverley, but Rupert must have found it.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Not knowing what the potion was, he simply tucked it away in his helmet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not more potions?! I've had enough of potions for now.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Relax, my friend. This potion is almost harmless. It is actually a particularly strong hair-tonic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh! Well, that would explain Rupert's beard then.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Yes, indeed! I also imagine poor Rupert never understood why his beard grew so alarmingly fast. Poor lad.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Anyway, I have removed the potion now and you may keep the helmet along with your rewards. Don't worry, it's quite harmless and the helmet should help you in your adventures.

sections.title: Talking to Sylas without a free inventory space

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Have you cut down the stalk?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, it's done.

lines.speaker: Sylas
lines.text: Great work, [player name]. Now, I have something for you, but you're going to need a space in your inventory. Speak to me again when you have made some room.
